data.title: ticket create bug: agent-generated bug descriptions omit required template headers -> validation warning emitted but ticket created with non-compliant structure
data.description:
## Incident Overview
During the 2026-04-18 evening session, multiple bug tickets were created by the orchestrating agent (Claude) during /dso:end-session. Ticket 0551-3244 received a post-creation warning: 'Bug description missing recommended headers: Actual Behavior'. The ticket was created successfully despite the violation — the warning is non-blocking.

## Expected Behavior
Bug tickets created by agents follow the bug-report-template.md structure exactly: all required sections present (## Incident Overview, ## Expected Behavior, ## Actual Behavior, ## Steps to Reproduce or equivalent, ## Fix Direction), and the title follows [Component]: [Condition] -> [Observed Result] format.

## Actual Behavior
Agents omit required sections — most commonly '## Actual Behavior' — and substitute free-form narrative headers ('## The Process Failure', '## Analogous gaps...'). The title format is followed loosely but the description structure is not. The post-creation warning is the only signal of non-compliance.

## Agent Rationalization (why this keeps happening)
The agent's rationalization, as observed and self-reported:

1. **Soft enforcement**: The warning is non-blocking. The ticket is created and the workflow continues. There is no retry loop, no hard failure, no forced re-prompt. Soft signals are reliably ignored by LLM agents under time/context pressure.

2. **Template not surfaced at generation time**: The agent writes the -d argument inline without re-reading bug-report-template.md first. The template path is known (CLAUDE.md references it) but agents treat it as optional context rather than a mandatory read-before-write gate.

3. **Prose quality over schema compliance**: When generating descriptions mid-session (especially during /dso:end-session under context pressure), the agent optimizes for completeness of thought rather than structural compliance. Narrative headers feel more readable and are generated faster than schema-matched ones.

4. **No in-context reminder at the creation callsite**: The ticket CLI does not print the required headers before accepting input. The agent has to self-impose the constraint, which it reliably fails to do under cognitive load.

## Fix Direction
- Make the validation warning blocking (exit non-zero) for missing required headers, or require --force to override
- Add a pre-creation prompt from the CLI that prints the required headers when creating bug type tickets
- Add a CLAUDE.md rule: 'Before .claude/scripts/dso ticket create bug, read plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/bug-report-template.md and verify all required sections are present'
- Alternatively, have the ticket CLI auto-scaffold the description with required section stubs when --description is not provided
